clinical_history: 84 year-old female status post cardioversion for atrial fibrillation.  The ECG is most consistent with which of the following rhythms?
diagnosis: This ECG shows probable ectopic atrial rhythm at rate about 70 bpm, although sinus rhythm with unusually leftward P wave axis is not excluded.  The PR interval is slightly prolonged (0.22 seconds) indicating first degree AV conduction abnormality.  Left atrial abnormality (biphasic P in V1 with prominent negative component) is present. The predominantly negative P waves in lead II and biphasic P waves in aVR strongly suggest an ectopic atrial focus following cardioversion in this case.
